verdicts.note: Direction rests on a single general pamphlet phrase ('delivering investments in housing') read as the public-investment pole, while the only concrete housing action in the dossier (HB 2228 building-code flexibility) is a supply/streamlining move toward the opposite pole. The +1 sign is plausible but the placement is an indirect inference from mixed evidence; low confidence is warranted.

Score rests on the Planned Parenthood endorsement [S1], a direct on-axis signal; medium is acceptable, but the 'equity- and access-oriented framing' language in the basis comes from the contest overview's characterization, not a candidate statement — keep magnitude at +1 and no higher.

The factual no-record claim is well cited [S1][S3], but placing him at the 'new voices/renewal' pole is an indirect inference from an absence of qualifications: the score's own basis concedes he articulates no renewal case, and repeat-candidate status undercuts an 'outsider energy' reading. Low confidence is the honest level, and it appropriately keeps this score out of alignment math so voters weighting 'new voices' are not matched to a candidate with no policy content.
